highlights.text: I wrote on Thursday at 4pm before the carnage: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
My lean is we work to 6794 to backtest where we broke down today, perhaps extend to 6805.” Friday morning, ES got to 6806 high of day exact.
It was at this point that the tariff “tape bomb” hit the market, broke down the range, and triggered - for the first time in what felt like weeks - my Bear Case.

I also wrote Thursday at 4pm: “Bear case tomorrow: No bear case until sub 6712-16 (megaphone failure) but there is a short-term bear case on the failure of the range at 6757 now.” We broke down the range, and down we went.
We don’t predict when it will come - it comes whenever we get the Failed Breakdown.” It didn’t take long on Sunday, as I stated Friday: “M y general lean is we can start back-testing on Monday.
Readers know though that “elevator down” days like Friday have a sibling.
Just like in physics where every action has an equal and opposite reaction, every “elevator down” sell in ES sees an equally violent snap back rally, induced by a Failed Breakdown.
I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “We also need to remember one of the core laws of ES, which is that after every “elevator down” sell, comes an equally violent squeeze.
By catalyst, readers know that ES spent all last week rangebound in a range mostly 6757 to 6805-07.

post_title: Bulls Bought The Dip As Expected In SPX. Dead Cat Bounce, Or Bottom? October 14 Plan
